title: Asynchronous Programming Quiz
description: Evaluate your understanding of Callbacks, Promises, and the modern industry standard: Async/Await for performance and readability in Node.js.

questions.question: Which and/or what is the primarily design philosophy behind 'Asynchronous Programming' in Node.js?
options: A system focused on 'Non-Blocking' I/O, allowing many tasks (like reading from a file) to run at once without stopping the application.

questions.question: Which and/or what was the primarily problem with early 'Callbacks' in Node.js when many were nested together?
Which modern JavaScript keyword is used inside an 'async' function to wait for a Promise to finish?
Is it possible to use try/catch blocks with Async/Await to handle errors in asynchronous code?
options: Yes, it is the standard and most readable way to handle errors in modern Node.js.
No, you can only use .catch() with Promises.

questions.question: Does Node.js allow you to use millions of pre-built packages and libraries to handle complex asynchronous tasks?
options: Yes, by providing a robust ecosystem for managing, sharing, and versioning your code.
